ddls:
CREATE TABLE "movies" (
  "movie_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each movie */,
  "title" TEXT /* Title of the movie */,
  "release_date" TEXT /* Release date of the movie */,
  "description" TEXT /* Brief description of the movie */,
  "runtime" INTEGER /* Duration of the movie in minutes */,
  "budget" REAL /* Budget of the movie */,
  "revenue" REAL /* Revenue generated by the movie */,
  "tagline" TEXT /* Tagline or slogan for the movie */,
  "poster_url" TEXT /* URL to the movie's poster image */,
  "trailer_url" TEXT /* URL to the movie's trailer */,
  "imdb_id" TEXT /* IMDB ID of the movie */,
  "tmdb_id" TEXT /* TMDb ID of the movie */,
  "production_company" TEXT /* Production company responsible for the movie */,
  "country" TEXT /* Country of origin for the movie */,
  "language" TEXT /* Primary language of the movie */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("movie_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "keywords" (
  "keyword_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each keyword */,
  "movie_id" INTEGER /* ID of the movie the keyword is associated with */,
  "keyword" TEXT /* Keyword itself (e.g., culture clash, future) */,
  "weight" REAL /* Weight or importance value for the keyword */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("keyword_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_keywords_movie_id FOREIGN KEY ("movie_id") REFERENCES movies ("movie_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "genres" (
  "genre_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each genre */,
  "genre_name" TEXT /* Name of the genre (e.g., action, comedy) */,
  "description" TEXT /* Brief description of the genre */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("genre_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "movie_genres" (
  "movie_id" INTEGER /* ID of the movie */,
  "genre_id" INTEGER /* ID of the genre */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("movie_id", "genre_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "directors" (
  "director_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each director */,
  "name" TEXT /* Name of the director */,
  "birth_date" TEXT /* Birth date of the director */,
  "nationality" TEXT /* Nationality of the director */,
  "biography" TEXT /* Brief biography of the director */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("director_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "movie_directors" (
  "movie_id" INTEGER /* ID of the movie */,
  "director_id" INTEGER /* ID of the director */,
  "role" TEXT /* Specific role of the director (e.g., director, co-director) */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("movie_id", "director_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "actors" (
  "actor_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each actor */,
  "name" TEXT /* Name of the actor */,
  "birth_date" TEXT /* Birth date of the actor */,
  "nationality" TEXT /* Nationality of the actor */,
  "biography" TEXT /* Brief biography of the actor */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("actor_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "movie_actors" (
  "movie_id" INTEGER /* ID of the movie */,
  "actor_id" INTEGER /* ID of the actor */,
  "character_name" TEXT /* Name of the character played by the actor */,
  "role" TEXT /* Specific role of the actor (e.g., lead, supporting) */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("movie_id", "actor_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "ratings" (
  "rating_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each rating */,
  "movie_id" INTEGER /* ID of the movie being rated */,
  "source" TEXT /* Source of the rating (e.g., IMDB, Rotten Tomatoes) */,
  "rating" REAL /* Rating value (e.g., 8.5, 92%) */,
  "review_count" INTEGER /* Number of reviews that contributed to the rating */,
  "date" TEXT /* Date when the rating was last updated */,
  "critic_score" REAL /* Critic score if available */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("rating_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_ratings_movie_id FOREIGN KEY ("movie_id") REFERENCES movies ("movie_id")
)
column_names: Duration of the movie in minutes
Tagline or slogan for the movie
URL to the movie's poster image
Production company responsible for the movie
Country of origin for the movie
ID of the movie the keyword is associated with
Weight or importance value for the keyword
Name of the genre (e.g., action, comedy)
Specific role of the director (e.g., director, co-director)
Name of the character played by the actor
Specific role of the actor (e.g., lead, supporting)
ID of the movie being rated
Source of the rating (e.g., IMDB, Rotten Tomatoes)
Number of reviews that contributed to the rating
Date when the rating was last updated
